report_name: Vidar Stealer Campaign Uses Fake Code Signing, Go Loaders, and File Inflation to Evade Detection

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Vidar Stealer Affiliate
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated cybercrime affiliate distributing the Vidar information stealer and XMRig cryptominer, leveraging the Factory-v3 loader-as-a-service framework. The actor targets a global audience, primarily in the U.S. and European Union.
threat_actors.goals: Financial gain through information theft and cryptocurrency mining.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The initial attack vector involves malvertising that lures victims searching for pirated software to download and manually execute a malicious loader, often packaged in a password-protected archive with a .bin extension.

attack_patterns.name: Code Signing
attack_patterns.description: The Go-based loaders were signed with a fabricated Authenticode certificate impersonating the legitimate German company JustWatch GmbH. The certificate was issued by a self-signed root CA created by the attackers to deceive users.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Side-Loading
attack_patterns.description: A malicious DLL named MpClient.dll, which exports functions from the legitimate Windows Defender file, is used to hijack the execution flow. When a legitimate Defender process loads the library, the malicious version is executed instead.

attack_patterns.name: Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion
attack_patterns.description: Attackers inflated loader binaries with hundreds of megabytes of null bytes, with one sample reaching 491 MB, to bypass automated analysis in sandboxes that often have file size limits.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses
attack_patterns.description: The Vidar stealer payload performs an in-memory AMSI bypass by loading amsi.dll, finding the AmsiScanBuffer function, and overwriting its initial bytes to force an error return, effectively disabling AMSI for the process.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The strings `amsi.dll` and `AmsiScanBuffer` used in the AMSI bypass are XOR-obfuscated to evade static detection. Additionally, the loader binaries are inflated with null bytes to evade sandbox analysis.

attack_patterns.name: Resource Hijacking
attack_patterns.description: The XMRig cryptocurrency miner is deployed alongside Vidar to covertly use victim system resources for mining Monero, leading to degraded system performance and increased electricity costs.

malware.name: Vidar
malware.description: A well-known information stealer malware designed to steal sensitive data such as browser credentials, cookies, and cryptocurrency wallets. In this campaign, it employs an in-memory AMSI bypass.

malware.name: XMRig
malware.description: An open-source cryptocurrency miner deployed by the attackers to covertly use victim system resources for mining Monero.

malware.name: Factory-v3
malware.description: A Go-based loader-as-a-service (MaaS) framework used to build and distribute the initial malware payloads. It employs techniques like code signing abuse, DLL sideloading, and file inflation to evade detection.

tools.name: Go
tools.description: The Go programming language was used to build the Factory-v3 loaders, as evidenced by embedded build metadata in the binaries.

identities.name: JustWatch GmbH
identities.description: A legitimate German company whose identity was impersonated on a fabricated Authenticode certificate used to sign malicious loaders.

identities.name: Consumers
identities.description: A primary target group for the Vidar and XMRig campaign, often lured by malvertising for cracked software.

identities.name: Small- to medium-sized businesses (SMBs)
identities.description: A primary target group for the Vidar and XMRig campaign.

infrastructure.name: Malvertising Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used to host malicious advertisements and redirect victims searching for pirated software to malicious download pages.

infrastructure.name: Vidar C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the Vidar stealer to exfiltrate stolen data.

infrastructure.name: XMRig Mining Pool Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Cryptocurrency mining pools that the XMRig miner connects to using the Stratum protocol.

indicators.name: Vidar Core Payload Hash
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Vidar Core Payload SHA256 Hash
id: 2b8c1f9a-3d1e-4b7c-8a2d-9f8e7c6b5a4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the Vidar core payload file hash identified in the Unit 42 report.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/vidar-stealer-xmrig-miner-campaign-analysis/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Hashes|contains: 'SHA256=7ed4a256e1d281cb4f194d13ff554fb280dafde0a67a18115ea038ea6c87d'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of the Vidar core payload identified by its SHA-256 hash.

indicators.name: MpClient.dll Sideloading Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential MpClient.dll Sideloading
id: 8c1d9e0a-4b7c-4f8e-9a2d-3b1c8f9a7d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the Windows Defender process (MsMpEng.exe) loading MpClient.dll from a non-standard system directory, which may indicate a DLL sideloading attack as seen in the Vidar campaign.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/vidar-stealer-xmrig-miner-campaign-analysis/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\MsMpEng.exe'
    ImageLoaded|endswith: '\MpClient.dll'
  filter:
    ImageLoaded|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\Windows Defender\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Defender\'
      - 'C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows Defender\'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects the Windows Defender process loading MpClient.dll from a non-standard location, a technique used by the Vidar campaign's loader.

indicators.name: XMRig Stratum Protocol Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: XMRig Cryptocurrency Mining Traffic
id: 1f9a8c1d-7d6e-4b7c-8a2d-9f8e3b1c8f9a
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network traffic using the Stratum protocol (stratum+tcp), which is characteristic of XMRig and other cryptocurrency miners.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/vidar-stealer-xmrig-miner-campaign-analysis/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1496
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|startswith: 'stratum+tcp://'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic characteristic of XMRig connecting to a Monero mining pool using the Stratum protocol.

indicators.name: Factory-v3 Loader PDB Path Artifact
indicators.pattern:
title: Factory-v3 Go Loader PDB Artifact
id: 5a4d2b8c-1f9a-4b7c-8a2d-9f8e7c6b3d1e
status: experimental
description: 'Detects files containing the PDB path string ''C:\\Users\\Administrator\\Desktop\\UpdateFactory'' associated with the Factory-v3 loader builder.'
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/vidar-stealer-xmrig-miner-campaign-analysis/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\UpdateFactory\compiler\'
  condition: keywords
indicators.description: Detects a PDB path artifact within binaries that is characteristic of the Factory-v3 loader builder.
